extraction_reasoning: The litigation tables distinguish matters against the Registered Office/company-related proceedings from matters against the Company, subsidiaries, Directors and Promoters; I counted only the against rows: criminal matters of 9 relating to the Registered Office, 1 against the Company, none against subsidiaries, 11 against Directors and 10 against Promoters, while regulatory actions are 3 against the Company and 2 against subsidiaries, and tax proceedings are 4 against the Company plus 2 against subsidiaries. I excluded all litigation filed by the Company, subsidiaries, Directors or Promoters and excluded Directors’ and Promoters’ tax/regulatory rows where nil; for the quantified group amount, I included ₹11.75 million and ₹2.20 million of tax, ₹29.50 million of subsidiary civil litigation, and ₹6.70 million of consumer complaints disclosed in the risk factor, totaling ₹50.15 million, or ₹5.015 crore, while excluding the MUR 0.79 million amount because it is not quantified in Indian rupees. The contingent-liabilities figure is the explicitly stated consolidated Total of ₹131.95 million as of December 31, 2018, converted to ₹13.195 crore rather than re-summing the line items. The concentration disclosures are company-wide revenue-from-operations percentages, but no top-five or top-ten customer percentage is disclosed for any fiscal period. The source monetary tables use ₹ million; conversion is million ÷ 10 to crore.
